skills.name: iOS Fundamentals
skills.description: Understanding the core structure and architecture of iOS, including its main layers and how apps interact with the system.
skills.notes: Covers Core OS, Core Services, and Cocoa Touch. Learn how iOS apps are structured and how the system manages resources and app lifecycle.

skills.name: Swift Programming
skills.description: Primary programming language for building iOS applications with modern syntax and safety features.
skills.notes: Learn variables, control flow, optionals, structs, classes, protocols, and Swift-specific features like ARC and type safety.

skills.name: Core Programming Concepts
skills.description: Essential programming concepts required for building scalable and maintainable applications.
skills.notes: Includes OOP, functional programming, memory management, error handling, and concurrency (GCD, async/await).

skills.name: Xcode & Development Environment
skills.description: Using Xcode IDE for building, debugging, and managing iOS applications.
skills.notes: Covers project setup, interface builder, debugging tools, breakpoints, simulators, and navigating project files.

skills.name: Version Control (Git)
skills.description: Managing source code and collaboration using Git and platforms like GitHub.
skills.notes: Learn commits, branching, merging, pull requests, and team collaboration workflows.

skills.name: UIKit
skills.description: Framework for building user interfaces using imperative programming.
skills.notes: Includes views, view controllers, storyboards, navigation, transitions, and handling user interactions.

skills.name: SwiftUI
skills.description: Modern declarative framework for building user interfaces across Apple platforms.
skills.notes: Covers views, modifiers, state management, data binding, and building responsive UI with less code.

skills.name: Architectural Patterns
skills.description: Design patterns used to structure iOS applications for scalability and maintainability.
skills.notes: Includes MVC, MVVM, VIPER, and TCA. Helps separate concerns and improve code organization.

skills.name: Data Persistence
skills.description: Techniques for storing and retrieving data locally on the device.
skills.notes: Includes Core Data, UserDefaults, Keychain, file system, and SQLite. Important for offline functionality.

skills.name: Networking
skills.description: Handling communication between the app and external servers or APIs.
skills.notes: Covers HTTP/HTTPS, REST APIs, URLSession, JSON parsing, and libraries like Alamofire.

skills.name: Concurrency & Multithreading
skills.description: Managing multiple tasks efficiently to keep apps responsive.
skills.notes: Includes Grand Central Dispatch (GCD), async/await, operation queues, and handling background tasks.

skills.name: Testing & Debugging
skills.description: Ensuring app quality through testing and identifying issues during development.
skills.notes: Includes unit testing (XCTest), UI testing, debugging tools, performance profiling, and crash analysis.

skills.name: Deployment & DevOps
skills.description: Building, distributing, and maintaining iOS applications in production environments.
skills.notes: Includes CI/CD (GitHub Actions, Jenkins), TestFlight, App Store submission, and App Store Optimization (ASO).
